sections.paragraph:
φῶς, φωτός, τό [Attic contraction of φάος ‘light’, Skt. assoc.]

—1. ‘that which serves as a revealing or disclosing medium’, light

—a. in the physical realm: Mt 17:2; Lk 8:16; Ac 12:7; 2 Cor 4:6; 6:14; Rv 18:23; as opp. of darkness Mt 4:16; 6:23; 10:27. In imagery ἐν τῷ φωτί = openly Mt 10:27; Lk 12:3. Derived from an extraterrestrial source: Ac 9:3; 12:7; 22:6, 9, 11; 26:13; 2 Cor 11:14; Rv 21:24.

—b. in non-physical sense:

—α. in general 1 J 7a.

—β. in connection with Jesus J 1:7; 9:5; 12:35f, 46.

—γ. in ref. to God Lk 2:32; J 8:12a; 1 Ti 6:16; 1 J 1:5, 7b.

—δ. of believers: w. focus on the effect of divine illumination displayed in them Mt 4:16; Lk 16:8; Ac 26:18; Ro 13:12; Eph 5:9, 13; Col 1:12; 1 Th 5:5; 1 Pt 2:9; 1 J 2:8; w. focus on their role as bearers of divine light Mt 5:14, 16; Ac 13:47; Ro 2:19.

—2. ‘that which emits illumination or radiance’, context-specific: torch/lamp Ac 16:29; fire Mk 14:54; Lk 22:56; sun J 11:9; heavenly bodies Js 1:17; eye Mt 6:23; Lk 11:35.
